_source.primary: SAP help.sap.com (Declustering in HCM Payroll; PCLn structure; ABAP data cluster documentation) for everything about SAP's own behaviour and the S/4HANA position. Cluster area lists come from a community reference document supplied by the user, because SAP does not publish them.
_source.provenance: Object names, IDs, note numbers and release levels are facts and are restated in our own words. No SAP prose is reproduced.
_source.areasCaveat: SAP does not enumerate the RELID areas of PCL1, PCL3 or PCL4 anywhere public. It tells you to read the fixed values of the RELID domain in your own Data Dictionary. The lists below are community-sourced and are the one part of this page we cannot stand behind — check SE11 if it matters.
notVerified: An 'AN — applicant notes' area on PCL3 is claimed by some third-party answers. No source we hold lists it, so it is omitted rather than guessed.

_common.title: How a cluster table works
_common.why: A cluster table combines records sharing a key into one physical row, written to the database compressed. Retrieval is very fast when you know the key, and impossible by any other route — the payload is a binary string, so no SELECT on it means anything and no external tool can interpret it.
_common.ddic: These are ordinary TRANSPARENT tables. The clustering is done by the application inside the CLUSTD field, not by the Dictionary. That distinction decides their S/4HANA fate — see below.

keyLayout.what: Cluster ID — which subarea of the table this row belongs to
Work area key. Its internal layout differs per cluster ID.
Row number, for when one cluster spans several rows
How much of CLUSTD this row actually uses
The payload — the serialised binary image of the ABAP tables and field strings

_common.identical: All four PCLn have the same structure. SAP states this explicitly.
_common.noPcl5: There is no PCL5. SAP consistently writes PCLn where n is 1, 2, 3 or 4.

parts.what: country grouping: 0 for international, otherwise the country indicator from T500L

includeConvention.discrepancy: Community code very often cites RPC2RX09 and RPC2CD09 instead, which do not fit SAP's published rule. Both forms are in circulation. Check what exists in your system rather than trusting either.
includeConvention.note: The INCLUDE defines both the work area key and the shape of what an IMPORT returns. Work-area structures follow the pattern PCnnn — PC200 is the payroll key (personnel number plus sequence number), PC261 the payroll directory.

macros.pattern: RP-IMP-C2-RX reads cluster RX from PCL2; RP-EXP-C4-LA writes cluster LA to PCL4. SAP's position is that only these macros should be used.
macros.buffered: The macro route needs the buffer includes RPPPXD00, RPPPXD10 and RPPPXM00. The macros themselves live in table TRMAC.
macros.authWarning: A bare IMPORT FROM DATABASE performs NO cluster authorisation check. The check only happens on the buffered macro route. Anything reading payroll data directly is bypassing the authorisation SAP intended.

_common.countryPattern: Payroll clusters follow R + the country grouping from T500L. RX is the international form, RD Germany, RU the United States. Every payroll country has its own, so treat this as a rule rather than looking for a fixed list.
_common.twoStep: Payroll results need two keys, personnel number and sequence number, and you do not know the sequence number up front. Read the directory first — HRPY_RGDIR is transparent and can simply be SELECTed — then read the result. Function module CD_EVALUATION_PERIODS works out which periods are relevant; each result carries status A (current), P (previous) or O (old).

_s4.headline: The table is unchanged. The right to run the application that uses it is what changed.

notAClusterTable.title: Not affected by the 1809 pool-and-cluster item
notAClusterTable.body: SAP ended support for pooled and cluster tables in S/4HANA 1809 (Note 2577406) — that item killed CDCLS, RFBLG and DOKCLU. It does NOT apply here. Those were Dictionary table clusters, which have no real database table of their own. PCL1–PCL4 are ordinary transparent tables that happen to hold an application-level data cluster in an LRAW field. No S/4HANA conversion turns PCL2 into transparent tables. Anyone telling you otherwise is conflating two different meanings of the phrase 'cluster table'.

compatibility.title: What actually changes: entitlement
compatibility.body: Classic ERP HCM runs inside S/4HANA only in Compatibility Mode, covered by Simplification List item Note 2273108 and licensed under the Compatibility Scope Matrix, Note 2269324. Those use rights were due to end 31 December 2025 and SAP extended them to 31 May 2026, describing it as a final transition period. The licensed successor is SAP HCM for S/4HANA, component S4HCM 100. This is the real S/4HANA question for anything touching PCL1–PCL4 — not the table, the entitlement.

declustering.title: Declustering — the only thing that makes payroll results relational
declustering.body: An OPTIONAL business function, switched on in SFW5. It writes payroll results to plain transparent tables IN ADDITION TO the cluster, synchronously during the payroll run. It is not a migration: PCL2 remains the system of record, the two are kept in sync, and you can switch declustering off and discard the transparent tables — but you cannot discard PCL2.
declustering.notAnS4Feature: Worth being precise about, because it is easy to mis-sell: this is an ECC-era feature, available since SAP_HR 6.04 / EhP4 SPS13. S/4HANA did not introduce it and does not turn it on for you.
declustering.mandatoryWhen: Optional in general — but MANDATORY if you run Payroll Control Center. SAP lists it as a required business function for PCC.

targets.convention: P + cluster file number + RELID + underscore + the internal table name. So P2RX_RT is PCL2, cluster RX, internal table RT.

limits: Only the main payroll cluster IDs are supported — RX, RA and CN. SAP states that RELIDs for special country-specific functions are NOT supported, so this is not a blanket 'all payroll results become transparent'.
There is already a transparent table HRPY_WPBP, but it does not carry the WPBP split, so declustering cannot use it and creates its own table instead.
Non-standard access to the clusters — direct SQL, unbuffered report access — is not supported. Hand-rolled cluster writers will silently desync the transparent tables.
No secondary indexes are shipped, because the tables are meant to live on columnar storage.

objects.what: Initial load — fills the transparent tables from what PCL2 already holds
Rebuilds missing declustered rows from the cluster
Deletes declustered data — explicitly NOT the data in PCLn
The time evaluation equivalent, for cluster B2

ecPayroll.title: Employee Central Payroll does not escape this
ecPayroll.body: A common claim is that SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll replaces the clusters with API-driven structures. It does not. EC Payroll is the classic ABAP payroll engine hosted by SAP — the identical declustering documentation is published under the EC Payroll product, naming the same HRPY_RGDIR, P2RX_RT and business functions. It writes PCL2 exactly as ECC does.

reporting.title: Reading results relationally
reporting.body: Declustering plus Payroll Control Center is what SAP actually documents. PCC validation rules are built as ordinary database joins across the declustered tables and the result directories. There is no delivered CDS view layer over payroll results that we could find — do not plan on one.

unverified: No SAP Note number specific to declustering could be established. We do not print one rather than invent one.
No Simplification List item for declustering appears to exist; it is a plain product feature.

PCL1.name: HR Cluster 1
PCL1.sapDesc: Basis for HR work area data — time data recording, incentive wage time tickets, and the supplementary texts attached to infotypes.
PCL1.desc: Holds HR working data that does not belong in an infotype record: time events from data collection terminals, incentive wage data, the personal calendar, travel expense data, and the long texts attached to infotype records.
PCL1.keyNote: For TX the work area key is PSKEY — personnel number, infotype, subtype, object ID, lock indicator, end date, start date, sequence number. That is why an infotype's long text is keyed the same way as the infotype record itself.

PCL2.name: HR Cluster 2
PCL2.sapDesc: Derived information — payroll results above all, plus every generated payroll schema.
PCL2.desc: The payroll and time evaluation results themselves, plus the generated schemas that produced them and the directory you need in order to find any of it.

areas.what: Cluster directory — read this first to get the sequence number
Payroll results, country-specific. n is the country grouping from T500L — RD is Germany, RU the United States.

PCL2.keyNote: For payroll results the work area key is PC200 — personnel number plus a sequence number. The country-independent part of the definition sits in one INCLUDE and the country-specific part in another, which is how one payroll engine serves every country.

PCL3.name: HR Cluster 3
PCL3.desc: Recruitment data: the applicant action log and time schedule, and the texts attached to applicant infotypes.

areas.what: Applicant action log and time schedule

PCL4.name: HR Cluster 4
PCL4.sapDesc: Change documents for HR master data and recruitment.
PCL4.desc: The audit trail. Every change to HR master data and applicant data is logged here, which makes it the table auditors ask about and the one nobody can read.
PCL4.keyNote: For LA the work area key is PC400 — a transaction class (A for master data), personnel number, infotype, the date and time of the change, and a sequence number.
